title: Crocanthemum nashii
intro: Crocanthemum nashii, also known as Rockrose or Nash's rockrose, is a perennial shrub native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Crocanthemum nashii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Crocanthemum nashii is classified within the family Cistaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the rockrose family. This family is part of the larger clade of Malvids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Malvaceae and Tiliaceae. The genus Crocanthemum is a group of shrubs and subshrubs native to eastern North America, and C. nashii is one of the species within this genus. C. nashii is specifically classified as a perennial shrub that is native to the southeastern United States. It is a member of the Rosidae clade, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes many other families.
